title: Announcement / update
compact: How to test an announcement / update
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Arrow keys: browses to announced/updated text
- Ctrl + tab: nothing happens because announcements must NOT be focusable
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Relevant action: the screenreader reads the announced/updated text
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: its purpose is clear and matches visible content
- Role: it has no role
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text content can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-ios/announcement/
gherkin: GIVEN THAT I am on a page with an announcement / update
1 - Keyboard for mobile & desktop
- WHEN I use an action that triggers an announcement / update I SEE the announcement / update appears
- THEN when I use the ctrl + tab key I SEE nothing happens because announcements must NOT be focusable
2 - Desktop screenreader
- WHEN I use a screenreader AND
- I use an action that triggers an announcement / update
- I HEAR its purpose is clear and matches visible content
- I HEAR it has no role
- THEN when I use the ctrl + tab key I HEAR nothing happens because announcements must NOT be focusable
3. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text content can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-ios/announcement/

title: Button
compact: How to test a button
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Cntrl+tab or arrow keys: Focus visibly moves to the button
- Spacebar: Activates the button
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: Focus moves to the element, expresses its name, role, state
- Doubletap: Activates the button
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: Its purpose is clear and matches visible label
- Role: It identifies itself as a button
- Group: Any visible label is read with the button in a single swipe
- State: It expresses its state (dimmed, selected, etc)
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information
- Increased contrast: focus outline becomes high contrast
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-ios/button/
gherkin: GIVEN THAT I am on a page with a button
1 - Keyboard for mobile & desktop
- WHEN I use the ctrl + tab key or arrow key to move focus to a button I SEE focus is visually indicated (strongly if high contrast enabled)
- THEN when I use the spacebar to activate the button I SEE the action occurs
2 - Desktop screenreader
- WHEN I use a screenreader AND
- I use the ctrl + tab key or arrow key to move focus to a button
- I HEAR Its purpose is clear and matches visible label
- I HEAR It identifies itself as a button
- I HEAR Any visible label is read with the button in a single swipe
- I HEAR It expresses its state (dimmed, selected, etc)
- THEN when I use the spacebar to activate the button I HEAR the action occurs
3. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information
- Increased contrast: focus outline becomes high contrast
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-ios/button/

title: Card box
compact: How to test a card box
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Arrow keys or ctrl + tab: focus visibly moves to any control inside the card box
- Spacebar: the control is selected
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves to any element inside the cardbox
- Doubletap: intended actions occur
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: its purpose is clear and matches visible content
- Role: any nested control expresses its role (card has no role)
- Group: content and controls are individually readable
- State: any nested control expresses its state
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text content can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-ios/card/
gherkin: GIVEN THAT I am on a page with a card box
1 - Keyboard for mobile & desktop
- WHEN I use the arrow keys or ctrl + tab to move focus to any nested control I SEE focus is strongly visually indicated
- THEN when I use the spacebar to activate the control I SEE the control can be accessed
2 - Desktop screenreader
- WHEN I use a screenreader AND
- I use the arrow keys or ctrl + tab to move focus to any nested control
- I HEAR its purpose is clear and matches visible content
- I HEAR any nested control expresses its role (card has no role)
- I HEAR content and controls are individually readable
- I HEAR any nested control expresses its state
- THEN when I use the spacebar to activate the control I HEAR the control can be accessed
3. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text content can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-ios/card/

title: Carousel slideshow
compact: How to test a carousel slideshow
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Ctrl + tab or arrow keys: focus visibly moves to slides or controls
- Spacebar: activates slides or controls
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves to the next slide or controls
- Custom finger swipe: focus moves to the next slide
- Single point control: focus moves to the next slide (ex: Next button)
- Doubletap: activates the slides or controls
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: the purpose of each item is clear and matches visible content
- Role: it identifies itself as "adjustable" plus any custom gestures (ex: Swipe up/down with one finger)
- Group: its index is announced (ex: 3 of 5)
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information
- Increased contrast: focus outline becomes high contrast
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-ios/carousel/
gherkin: GIVEN THAT I am on a page with a carousel slideshow
1 - Keyboard for mobile & desktop
- WHEN I use the ctrl + tab key or arrow key to move focus to a slide or control I SEE focus is visually indicated (strongly if high contrast enabled)
- THEN when I use the spacebar to activate the slide or control I SEE the action occurs
2 - Desktop screenreader
- WHEN I use a screenreader AND
- I use the ctrl + tab key or arrow key to move focus to a slide or control
- I HEAR the purpose of each item is clear and matches visible content
- I HEAR it identifies itself as "adjustable" plus any custom gestures (ex: Swipe up/down with one finger)
- I HEAR its index is announced (ex: 3 of 5)
- THEN when I use the spacebar to activate the slide or control I HEAR the action occurs
3. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information
- Increased contrast: focus outline becomes high contrast
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-ios/carousel/

title: Checkbox
compact: How to test a checkbox
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Arrow keys or ctrl + tab: focus visibly moves to the checkbox
- Spacebar: element toggles between states
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves to the element, expresses its name, role, state (only if selected)
- Doubletap: element toggles between states
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: its purpose is clear and matches visible label
- Role: it identifies itself as a button or switch button
- Group: any visible label is read with the checkbox in a single swipe
- State: it expresses its state (selected, checked, unchecked, dimmed)
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information
- Increased contrast: focus outline becomes high contrast
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-ios/checkbox/

1 - Keyboard for mobile & desktop
- WHEN I use the arrow keys or ctrl + tab to move focus to a checkbox I SEE focus is visually indicated (strongly if high contrast enabled)
- THEN when I use the spacebar to activate the checkbox I SEE the state is changed
2 - Desktop screenreader
- WHEN I use a screenreader AND
- I use the arrow keys or ctrl + tab to move focus to a checkbox
- I HEAR its purpose is clear and matches visible label
- I HEAR it identifies itself as a button or switch button
- I HEAR any visible label is read with the checkbox in a single swipe
- I HEAR it expresses its state (selected, checked, unchecked, dimmed)
- THEN when I use the spacebar to activate the checkbox I HEAR the state is changed
3. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information
- Increased contrast: focus outline becomes high contrast
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-ios/checkbox/

title: Date picker
compact: How to test a date picker
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Arrow keys or ctrl + tab: focus visibly moves to the picker
- Spacebar: expands/collapses picker
- Arrow keys: selects options
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves to the picker, expresses its name, role, state
- Doubletap: picker displays options
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: its purpose is clear and matches visible label
- Role: it identifies itself as a button
- Group: any visible label is read with the picker in a single swipe
- State: it expresses its value (and state if disabled/dimmed)
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information
- Increased contrast: focus outline becomes high contrast
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-ios/date-picker/
gherkin: GIVEN THAT I am on a page with a date picker
1 - Keyboard for mobile & desktop
- WHEN I use the arrow keys or ctrl + tab to move focus to a picker I SEE focus is visually indicated (strongly if high contrast enabled)
- THEN when I use the spacebar I SEE the picker opens
- THEN when I use the arrow key I SEE the selection changes
2 - Desktop screenreader
- WHEN I use a screenreader AND
- I use the arrow keys or ctrl + tab to move focus to a picker
- I HEAR its purpose is clear and matches visible label
- I HEAR it identifies itself as a button
- I HEAR any visible label is read with the picker in a single swipe
- I HEAR it expresses its value (and state if disabled/dimmed)
- THEN when I use the spacebar I HEAR the picker opens
- THEN when I use the arrow key I HEAR the selection changes
3. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information
- Increased contrast: focus outline becomes high contrast
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-ios/date-picker/

title: Alert dialog
compact: How to test an alert dialog
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Launch button: focus visibly moves to the open dialog itself
- Arrow keys or ctrl + tab: focus visibly moves to interactive controls in the dialog
- Escape: if dismissible, the dialog closes and returns focus to the button that launched it
- Space: any buttons are activated
- Enter: any buttons or links are activated
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves within the dialog and doesn't enter the rest of the page.
- Doubletap: this typically activates most elements.
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: the dialog describes its purpose or title on launch
- Role: it identifies itself as a dialog
- Group: when closed, focus returns to the launch button
- State: when open, content behind the dialog remains inert
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text content can resize up to 200% without losing information
- Increased contrast: focus outline becomes high contrast
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-ios/dialog-alert/
gherkin: GIVEN THAT I am on a page with an alert dialog
1 - Keyboard for mobile & desktop
- WHEN I use the arrow keys or ctrl + tab to move focus to the launch button AND THEN use spacebar to activate the button I SEE the dialog opens
- THEN when I use the arrow keys I SEE content in the dialog is browsed in meaningful order and does not leave the dialog
- THEN when I use the ctrl + tab key I SEE focus moves to interactive controls in the dialog
- THEN when I use  I SEE focus returns to the launch button
2 - Desktop screenreader
- WHEN I use a screenreader AND
- I use the arrow keys or ctrl + tab to move focus to the launch button AND THEN use spacebar to activate the button
- I HEAR the dialog describes its purpose or title on launch
- I HEAR it identifies itself as a dialog
- I HEAR when closed, focus returns to the launch button
- I HEAR when open, content behind the dialog remains inert
- THEN when I use the arrow keys I HEAR content in the dialog is browsed in meaningful order and does not leave the dialog
- THEN when I use the ctrl + tab key I HEAR focus moves to interactive controls in the dialog
- THEN when I use  I HEAR focus returns to the launch button
3. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text content can resize up to 200% without losing information
- Increased contrast: focus outline becomes high contrast
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-ios/dialog-alert/

- Launch button: focus visibly moves to the open dialog itself
- Arrow keys or ctrl + tab: focus visibly moves to interactive controls in the dialog, starting with the first interactive control (typically close button)
- Escape: if dismissible, the dialog closes and returns focus to the button that launched it
- Space: any buttons are activated
- Enter: any buttons or links are activated
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves within the dialog and doesn't enter the rest of the page.
- Doubletap: this typically activates most elements.
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: the dialog describes its purpose or title on launch
- Role: it identifies itself as a dialog
- Group: when closed, focus returns to the launch button
- State: when open, content behind the dialog remains inert
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text content can resize up to 200% without losing information
- Increased contrast: focus outline becomes high contrast
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-ios/dialog-modal/

title: Heading
compact: How to test a heading
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Arrow keys: browses to headings
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: the screenreader reads the heading (and if used, its level)
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: its purpose is clear and matches visible content
- Role: it identifies itself as a heading
- Group: (if heading levels are used) it is logically structured
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text content can resize up to 200% without losing information
- Increased contrast: focus outline becomes high contrast
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-ios/heading/

2 - Desktop screenreader

- Swipe: focus moves through the content

3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: its purpose is clear (typically from a heading above)
- Group: content item index is announced (ex: 3 of 5)
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-ios/horizontal-scroll/

1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Arrow keys: ignores/skips the image completely
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: ignores/skips the image completely
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: it ignores/skips the image completely
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-ios/image-decorative/

title: Number input field
compact: How to test a number input field
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Arrow keys or ctrl + tab: focus visibly moves to the input
- Spacebar: selects input field
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves to the input, expresses its name, role, state then any instructions
- Doubletap: numeric keyboard appears appears to edit
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: its purpose is clear and matches visible label
- Role: it identifies itself as text field
- Group: its visible label and instructions are read with the input in focus
- State: if applicable, it expresses its state (required, dimmed, invalid)
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: Text can resize up to 200% without losing information
- Increased contrast: focus outline becomes high contrast
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-ios/input-number/

- WHEN I use the ctrl + tab or arrow keys to move focus to the input I SEE focus is visually indicated (strongly if high contrast enabled)
2 - Desktop screenreader
- WHEN I use a screenreader AND
- I use the ctrl + tab or arrow keys to move focus to the input
- I HEAR purpose is clear and matches visible label
- I HEAR identifies itself as text field
- I HEAR visible label and instructions are read with the input in focus
- I HEAR if applicable, it expresses its state (required, dimmed, invalid)
3. Device OS settings
- Text resize: Text can resize up to 200% without losing information
- Increased contrast: focus outline becomes high contrast
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-ios/input-website/

title: iOS app view
compact: How to test an ios app view
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Arrow keys or ctrl + tab: enters the page and focuses interactive elements
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves within page
- Orientation: content is accessible in landscape or portrait orientation
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: the page has a unique meaningful title heading
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-ios/ios-view/

title: Picker button
compact: How to test a picker button
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Arrow keys or ctrl + tab: focus visibly moves to the picker
- Spacebar: expands/collapses picker
- Arrow keys: selects options
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves to the picker, expresses its name, role, state
- Doubletap: picker displays options
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: its purpose is clear and matches visible label
- Role: it identifies itself as a button
- Group: any visible label is read with the picker in a single swipe
- State: it expresses its value (and state if disabled/dimmed)
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information
- Increased contrast: focus outline becomes high contrast
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-ios/picker-button/

title: Pop-up button
compact: How to test a pop-up button
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Arrow keys or ctrl + tab: focus visibly moves to the button
- Spacebar: expands/collapses popup button
- Arrow keys: selects options
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves to the button, expresses its name, role, state
- Doubletap: button displays options
- Swipe while open: navigates options
- Doubletap while open: selects options
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: its purpose is clear and matches visible label
- Role: it identifies itself as a popup button
- Group: any visible label is read with the button in a single swipe
- State: it expresses its state (dimmed, chosen selection)
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information
- Increased contrast: focus outline becomes high contrast
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-ios/pop-up-button/

title: Popover tooltip
compact: How to test a popover tooltip
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Arrow keys or ctrl + tab: focus visibly moves to the button
- Spacebar: expands/collapses popover
- Escape: closes the popover
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves to the button, expresses its name, role
- Doubletap: button displays popover
- Swipe again: browses popover
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: its purpose is clear and matches visible label
- Role: it identifies itself as a button
- Group: any visible label is read with the button in a single swipe
- State: it expresses its state (expanded)
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information
- Increased contrast: focus outline becomes high contrast
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-ios/popover/

title: Progress gauge
compact: How to test a progress gauge
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Arrow keys: browses to gauge
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: the screenreader reads the gauge and announces any current dynamic value (ex: Downloading receipt 12%)
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: its purpose is clear and matches visible content
- Role: it has no role
- State: the screenreader reads the gauge and announces any current dynamic value (ex: Downloading receipt 12%)
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text content can resize up to 200% without losing information
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-ios/progress-gauge/

2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves to the element, expresses its name, role, state (only if selected)
- Doubletap: element toggles between states
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: its purpose is clear and matches visible label
- Role: it identifies itself as a button
- Group: any visible label is read with the button in a single swipe
- State: it expresses its state (selected, dimmed)
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information
- Increased contrast: focus outline becomes high contrast

title: Search input field
compact: How to test a search input field
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Arrow keys or ctrl + tab: focus visibly moves to the input
- Spacebar: selects input, auto-suggestions
- Ctrl + tab or arrow keys: focus moves to auto-suggestions
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves to the input, expresses its name, role (and state if disabled/dimmed)
- Doubletap: keyboard appears appears to edit
- Keyboard: number of suggestions is announced
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: purpose is clear and matches visible label
- Role: identifies itself as search field
- Group: visible label is read with the input in focus
- Suggestions: number of suggestions is announced when typing
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: Text can resize up to 200% without losing information
- Increased contrast: focus outline becomes high contrast
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-ios/search/

title: Segmented control
compact: How to test a segmented control
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Ctrl + tab: focus visibly moves to the button
- Arrow keys: navigate through group
- Spacebar: activates the button
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves to the element, expresses its state, if applicable
- Doubletap: activates the button
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: purpose is clear and matches visible label
- Role: identifies as a button
- Group: visible label (if any) is grouped or associated with the button in a single swipe
- State: expresses its state (selected/disabled/dimmed)
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: this element is exempt from text resizing requirements
- Increased contrast: focus outline becomes high contrast
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-ios/segmented-control/

title: Slider
compact: How to test a slider
1. Test keyboard only, then keyboard + screenreader
- Arrow keys or ctrl + tab: focus moves visibly to the slider
- Left/right arrow-keys: increase/decrease slider value one step
2. Test mobile screenreader gestures
- Swipe: focus moves to the input
- Swipe up/down: value increase / decrease one step
3. Listen to screenreader output on all devices
- Name: its name describes the purpose of the control and matches the visible label
- Role: it identifies itself as "adjustable"
- Group: its visible label is read with the input
- State: it expresses its current value
4. Device OS settings
- Text resize: text can resize up to 200% without losing information
- Increased contrast: focus outline becomes high contrast
Full information: https://www.atomica11y.com/accessible-ios/slider/
